data.title: Nurix reports new data at ESMO on NX-1607, a CBL-B inhibitor, showing single-agent activity across multiple tumors.
data.description: NX-1607 has displayed peripheral immune activation that aligns with the profile of an active immune-oncology drug. This drug operates through a unique immune checkpoint mechanism separate from PD-1/PD-L1 treatments. These findings signify the potential of NX-1607 in the field of immune-oncology, showcasing its distinct mechanism of action.

data.title: Corbus Pharmaceuticals to present CRB-701 data at ESMO25, with results from 167 patients, 122 evaluable for efficacy, across various tumor types.
data.description: Corbus Pharmaceuticals Holdings, Inc. revealed that new data from its Phase 1/2 clinical study of CRB-701 (SYS6002) will be showcased at the 2025 European Society for Medical Oncology Congress. The poster, titled “Phase 1/2 study of CRB-701 (SYS6002) in patients with urothelial and non-urothelial solid tumors” by Perez et al, is scheduled for presentation on October 19, 2025, in Berlin, Germany. Data collected until September 1, 2025, from 167 patients will be shared, with 122 of them being evaluable for efficacy. The study focused on patients with head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC, n=41), cervical cancer (n=37), and locally advanced/metastatic urothelial (mUC, n=23) tumors. Additionally, 21 patients with other solid tumor types participated in the dose escalation phase.

data.title: Olema Oncology reveals data from Palazestrant plus Ribociclib trial for ER+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer at ESMO 2025.
data.description: Olema Oncology recently revealed fresh findings from the Phase 1b/2 clinical trial of Palazestrant combined with Ribociclib in patients with estrogen receptor-positive and HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer during the ESMO 2025 conference. The trial showcased promising results, suggesting significant efficacy of the combination therapy. This development could potentially enhance treatment options for individuals with this form of advanced breast cancer. The study's specifics demonstrated an increase in overall response rate, with X% of participants showing positive responses to the treatment. These results signify a breakthrough in cancer research and offer hope for improved outcomes for patients battling this aggressive disease.

data.title: Florida Cancer Specialists & Research Institute presents promising targeted therapy results at ESMO 2025 with abstracts including FCS research.
data.description: A presentation will discuss the preliminary findings of a pioneering clinical trial for a targeted therapy aimed at treating advanced or metastatic solid tumors. The abstracts presented at the ESMO 2025 conference will showcase research related to FCS, with notable references including 965P, 967P, 981P, 1014eP, 1031eTiP, 1068P, 1070P, 1073P, 1073P, 1113P, 1153P, 1166P, 1172P, etc.
Zelenskiy: Russia scared of Tomahawks, aware of potential combined with Ukraine's other weapons

data.title: Dow increases by nearly 600 points; Nasdaq shows strength; Tesla and Nvidia stage rebounds in Monday's rally.
data.description: During Monday's market rally, the Nasdaq emerged as a standout performer. Gold and silver stocks demonstrated significant strength. The Dow managed to recover nearly 600 points. Tesla and Nvidia saw impressive rebounds. This information was shared in live coverage of the stock market today.

data.title: Hadrian Capital Partners to sell 50 million CDIs of Capstone Copper Corp at A$13.75 each for proceeds of ~A$687,500,000. Settlement 10/14/2025.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on October 9, 2025, Hadrian Capital Partners Inc. revealed that they, along with Hadrian Capital Partners (No. 1) Inc., have engaged in a block trade deal with Macquarie Capital (Australia) Limited and Citigroup Global Markets Australia Pty Ltd. Under this agreement, Hadrian will sell and the Managers will underwrite the disposal of 50,000,000 CHESS depository interests (CDIs), equivalent to common shares (Shares) of Capstone Copper Corp., priced at A$13.75 per CDI. This transaction will result in gross proceeds of around A$687,500,000 for Hadrian. The settlement for the CDIs in question is expected on or around October 14, 2025 (Australia time).

data.title: Dow Jones Futures up; Taiwan Semi sales strong after Nvidia's CEO lift S&P 500, Delta Air earnings rose.
data.description: Strong sales were reported by Taiwan Semiconductor following a boost in the S&P 500 index by Nvidia CEO Huang. Delta Air saw an increase in earnings as well. Dow Jones Futures showed a rise in numbers. Taiwan Semiconductor, a chipmaker for Nvidia, reported robust sales, while Delta Air reported strong earnings.
THEON secures a substantial €300M Senior Facility Agreement in the latest development.

data.title: Congress' funding stalemate could prevent Labor Department employees from updating data accurately, impacting economic updates.
data.description: As Congress continues to be deadlocked in debates over government funding, there is a growing risk that furloughed employees at the Labor Department may not be able to access critical data. This could result in inaccurate updates regarding the current economic status. The longer this stalemate persists, the higher the likelihood that essential information, impacting economic decisions, may be delayed or unavailable. With each passing day, the urgency to reach a resolution becomes more apparent, to ensure the smooth operation of crucial departments and provide accurate updates on the economy's performance.

data.title: President imposes tariffs on imported timber, lumber, and furniture, escalating tensions with China.
data.description: The president has announced plans to escalate the trade dispute with China by imposing tariffs on various goods, such as timber, lumber, and kitchen cabinets. These tariffs went into effect on Tuesday, with the potential for further expansion of the trade war in the near future.

data.title: AI-powered data centers are driving a US power boom, with global data center electricity usage projected to grow 175% by 2030. US alone expected to see total electricity demand rise by 2.6% annually through 2030.
data.description: A surge in artificial intelligence infrastructure is driving a significant increase in power demand in the U.S., as per Goldman Sachs projections. The growth in electricity demand is unprecedented since the 1990s and is creating new investment opportunities in the energy and tech sectors. Global data center electricity usage, encompassing AI and non-AI operations, is predicted to surge by 175% by 2030 compared to 2023, a notable uptick from the previous estimate of 165%. This growth is akin to incorporating a top 10 power-consuming country into the global grid. In the U.S. alone, total electricity demand is set to rise by 2.6% annually until 2030, marking an acceleration of +1.2 percentage points attributable to data center expansion. Historically, U.S. power demand growth has seldom surpassed 2% over the last twenty years. Analyst Brian Singer, CFA at Goldman Sachs, highlighted these trends in a recent note.

data.title: Goldman Sachs analyst downgrades PayPal, Marqeta stocks citing downside risks to 2026 forecasts. PayPal stock forecasted at $70.
data.description: Goldman Sachs analyst, Will Nance, recently downgraded PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL) and Marqeta, Inc. (NASDAQ: MQ) due to potential downside risks to their 2026 forecasts. This move comes as investor sentiment turns cautious leading up to the fourth quarter. Nance highlighted concerns such as tougher year-end comparisons post last year’s uptick in spending, renewed challenges faced by lower-income consumers, and a shift away from cyclical sectors towards tech giants in terms of investment. Despite stable third-quarter fundamentals reflecting consistent spending patterns, the analyst anticipates a focus on potential reductions in 2026 estimates. Specifically, PayPal was downgraded to Sell from Neutral with a target price of $70, with management hints indicating a slowdown in branded checkout growth. For a comprehensive report, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: AZZ stock is falling after reporting weaker-than-expected Q2 FY26 results with $417.3 million revenue, missing $426.2 million consensus. Metal Coatings sales rose 10.8% to $190.0 million while Precoat Metals sales declined 4.3% to $227.3 million. Adjusted diluted EPS increased by 13.1% year-over-year.
data.description: Shares of AZZ Inc. (NYSE: AZZ) are experiencing a premarket decline as the company released its second-quarter FY26 results, falling short of expectations. The revenue saw a 2.0% increase from the previous year, reaching $417.3 million, which failed to meet the anticipated $426.2 million. In terms of segment performance, Metal Coatings sales surged by 10.8% year-over-year to $190.0 million, attributed to higher volumes. On the other hand, Precoat Metals witnessed a 4.3% decline in sales on a yearly basis, amounting to $227.3 million, due to challenging end markets, particularly in building construction, HVAC, and appliances. The adjusted diluted EPS also saw a 13.1% increase year-over-year.

data.title: Helen Of Troy Limited (NASDAQ: HELE) reports 51% drop in adjusted earnings per share, with shares dropping 16% premarket, due to cost pressures and tariffs. Sales decline 8.9% to $431.8 million in Q2.
data.description: Helen Of Troy Limited's shares (NASDAQ: HELE) dropped by more than 16% in premarket trading on Thursday following a report of a 51% decrease in adjusted earnings per share for the second quarter. The company warned of ongoing cost pressures and disruptions related to tariffs that are anticipated to impact the fiscal year 2026 results. The adjusted earnings per share plummeted by 51.2% year-over-year to 59 cents, surpassing the analyst consensus of 53 cents. Quarterly sales also saw a decline of 8.9% year-over-year to $431.8 million, surpassing the expected $418.8 million. The disappointing results were attributed to significant business disruptions and cost challenges that are likely to persist throughout the year. Home & Outdoor net sales experienced a 13.7% year-over-year decrease.

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs lose $1.2B amid market dip, but Schwab optimistic as its clients possess 20% of US crypto ETPs.
data.description: This week, Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) experienced a loss of $1.22 billion due to the decline in BTC value. On a positive note, Schwab announced that its clients now possess a significant 20% share of all cryptocurrency exchange-traded products (ETPs) in the United States.

data.title: Judge to expand order preventing layoffs as Trump plans more cuts; agencies considered layoffs in court, but judge broadens ban.
data.description: In recent court filings, officials at various agencies had hinted at the potential for layoffs, but a federal judge has intervened by expanding an original prohibition on the layoffs. This decision comes as a relief to many employees facing uncertainty about their jobs.

data.title: Roche's evERA trial showed giredestrant improves PFS by 44-62% in ER-positive breast cancer patients, presented at ESMO 2025.
data.description: Roche has reported positive results from the phase III evERA Breast Cancer study, showing that giredestrant in combination with everolimus reduced the risk of disease progression or death by 44% in the intention-to-treat population and by 62% in ESR1-mutated populations, compared to standard endocrine therapy plus everolimus. The study focuses on people with ER-positive, HER2-negative, locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer who were previously treated with CDK 4/6 inhibitor and endocrine therapy. This is the first successful phase III trial to compare a selective oestrogen receptor degrader regimen with standard care. The results will be presented at the European Society for Medical Oncology Congress 2025, and Roche plans to share the findings with health authorities to expedite the availability of this potential treatment option.

data.title: SCG presents clinical results of SCG142 for HPV-related carcinoma at ESMO 2025.	SCG focuses on immunotherapies for diseases & cancers.
data.description: A Singapore-based biotechnology company called SCG Cell Therapy Pte Ltd has recently shared the initial clinical data for their product SCG142. This company focuses on developing advanced immunotherapies for infectious diseases and related cancers. The data presented marks the first time SCG142 has been tested in humans. The presentation was made on October 18, 2025, and no specific results or numbers were mentioned in the announcement.

data.title: Kevin O'Leary advises Trump to protect American Dream, not pick sectors. Capitalism is best system; less gov't is better. U.S. top for entrepreneurship with number one export being American dream.
data.description: Investor Kevin O'Leary recently advised President Donald Trump to prioritize safeguarding the American dream over focusing on specific industries. O'Leary emphasized the effectiveness of capitalism despite its imperfections, advocating for minimal government intervention in the economy. In a video shared on social media, O'Leary highlighted that U.S. presidents should nurture the nation's entrepreneurial spirit rather than favoring particular sectors. He stressed that the U.S. economy's success over the past two centuries signifies competence in decision-making. O'Leary emphasized that the U.S. is a leading hub for entrepreneurship globally, with the American dream being its principal export. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
